description: You are the fleet operations director at Prestige Executive Transport, a premium chauffeured car service for corporate clients. A driver is starting the evening shift and their GPS device needs to be configured to meet company standards. Navigation must guide the driver to arrive at destinations on the correct side of the street for white-glove service. The display must use the dark color scheme at all times to prevent windshield glare during night operations. Toll roads must be available as routing options, never excluded. The compass bearing must be permanently visible on the screen for orientation in complex environments like hotel loops and private estates. Configure the GPS to output coordinates in degrees-minutes-seconds (DMS) format to align with the concierge team's staging area protocols.
